title: Array Element Pair Sum Checker with Off-by-One Error

statement.markdown: Write a function that takes an array of integers as input and checks if it satisfies the condition that the sum of every pair of elements is greater than the sum of the remaining elements. If the array has an odd length, consider the middle element separately. The function should return True if the condition is satisfied, and False otherwise.
statement.inputFormat: Input will be a single line string containing a space-separated list of integers.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return True if the condition is satisfied, and False otherwise.
statement.notes: The input array will contain at least one element. The input array will not be empty.

examples.explanation: The sum of every pair of elements (1+2, 2+3, 3+4, 4+5) is greater than the sum of the remaining elements (5, 1, 3, 4).
The sum of the pair of elements (1+2) is not greater than the sum of the remaining elements (3, 4).

hints.text: Check the condition for pairs of elements and the remaining elements separately.

editorial.title: Array Element Pair Sum Checker
editorial.intuition: The condition can be checked by iterating over the pairs of elements and the remaining elements separately.
editorial.bruteForce: We can use nested loops to iterate over the pairs of elements and the remaining elements.
editorial.betterSolution: We can use a single loop to iterate over the pairs of elements and the remaining elements.
editorial.optimalSolution: We can use a single loop to iterate over the pairs of elements and the remaining elements, and use a variable to keep track of the sum of the remaining elements.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The condition is satisfied if and only if the sum of every pair of elements is greater than the sum of the remaining elements.
editorial.dryRun: Let's consider an example: [1, 2, 3, 4]. We can form pairs (1, 2), (2, 3), and (3, 4). The remaining elements are 4. The sum of the pairs is 1+2+2+3+3+4, and the sum of the remaining elements is 4.
